phase3_findings.description: second line-wrapped 'arm 2' cross-reference (documented_falsehood) — promoted from iter-2 conv-shadow
describe-plugin-compose.sh lacks a guard-class-2 grep pin
grep-derived helper-trust discriminator (guard-class-2 selection via non-preflight tool)
computed baseline SPOF on mid-step mktemp abort (fails loud)
HELPER || true swallows stderr-less exec failure

fix_decisions.claim_text: grep -Eq '"name":"devflow"' discriminator that gates materializing+executing the base-ref scripts/ copy is a guard-class-2 selection via a non-preflight PATH tool
fix_decisions.evidence: Verified against the codebase (receiving-code-review verify-before-implementing): 2 of the 3 identical discriminators in devflow-runner.yml are PRE-EXISTING (git blame line 311 = commit 22ae53d6, 2026-07-10, the #402/#404 deny-floor) — this PR added exactly 1 (line 347) and its own comment says 'same plugin.json-name discriminator baseprovision uses', i.e. it deliberately mirrors the established pattern. The grep fails CLOSED (grep-absence -> pipe non-zero -> elif false -> rank-2/baseline), so the specific hazard guard-class-2 guards ('tool absent -> empty value -> WRONG thing selected') does not occur — it selects the SAFE default. Converting only the PR-added instance to jq/python3 would desync three sibling discriminators and reach into pre-existing deny-floor code; changing the discriminator mechanism belongs in a dedicated uniform change across all three sites + filter-runner-tools.sh, not a one-off desync in #505. Latent wrapped-'name'-line -> silent-baseline-no-breadcrumb concern is identical across all three (pre-existing) and recorded as a follow-up.

fix_decisions.claim_text: plugins/plugin_marketplaces are now computed step outputs; a mid-step mktemp/set-e abort yields empty inputs -> claude-code-action runs with no plugins incl devflow@
fix_decisions.evidence: over-grade shape 1 (fail-closed/loud graded above blast radius): the reviewer itself states 'Loud, not silent (the job fails; the downstream Run Claude Code step does not run) ... an availability/robustness regression, not a silent failure'. A mktemp abort under set -euo pipefail fails the JOB loudly (a bounded stop), never silently ships an empty plugin surface. The baseline-first hardening is a reasonable optional improvement but restructuring the compose across 3 tiers to guard a rare loud-failing mktemp is disproportionate; recorded as advisory.

fix_decisions.claim_text: bash $HELPER ... || true can swallow a stderr-less exec failure
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion; the helper always exits 0 and exec failures almost always write stderr (-> DEFECT). Low value; advisory.

fix_decisions.claim_text: review-tier inline compose-routing branch-selection only grep-pinned (extract-into-helper)
fix_decisions.evidence: Same deferred follow-up as iter-2 (F2-inline-classification-extract); pr-test-analyzer graded it Important both passes but explicitly 'not a blocker, rating ~6, accepted limitation'. Git-materialization-coupled extraction is a scope-expanding test-architecture refactor for a dedicated issue, not #505. Recorded evidence, not auto-fixed.

convergence_inputs.note: both fixes are comment + test-pin only; 0 product/runtime code lines changed

skipped_checks.reason: working tree dirty (grades committed HEAD) — resolves post-commit / runs in CI

stale_prose_check.rows: no STALE rows at post-iter-3 HEAD

test_first_gate.evidence: pin returns 0 today; would go RED on a leading tr/sed/wc/cut/head added to the helper's entry-join loop

sweep_evidence.note: class-generalization sweep: after fixing the wrapped 'arm 2', swept ALL 'arm N' refs #-and-whitespace-normalized across all three workflows + describe-plugin-compose.sh — only correct refs remain (runner 'arm 3' x2, DPC header self-definitions). No sibling false arm-refs left.
